case_title: WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. VS. MARIA NIEVES

docket_entries.description: DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A.'S MOTION TO DEEM RFAS ADMITTED. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO DEEM REQUESTS FOR ADMISSIONS SET ONE ADMITTED IS UNOPPOSED AND GRANTED. THE PLAINTIFF HAS SPECIFICALLY WAIVED ITS STATUTORY RIGHT TO MONETARY SANCTIONS. A COPY OF THIS ORDER WITH NOTICE OF ENTRY TO WHICH REQUEST FOR ADMISSIONS WHICH IS THE SUBJECT OF THIS ORDER IS ATTACHED SHALL BE SERVED ON THE DEFENDANT WITHIN FIFTEEN DAYS. THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER REPEATING VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING AND MUST E-MAIL IT TO THE JUDGE PRO TEM AT SBS@SBSLAWSF.COM JUDGE PRO TEM: STEVEN STEIN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. =(302/JPT)

Off Calendar Jan-29-2021 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Nov-18-2020 Set For Case Management Conference On Feb-17-21 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT / Notice Of Motion & Motion For Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Oct-29-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance is made in this matter.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

PLAINTIFF WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication is GRANTED. No opposition filed, and good cause shown.

Written order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: proceedings not reported.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Deem Rfas Admitted
Off Calendar Sep-15-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A.'s Motion To Deem Rfas Admitted.

Plaintiff's motion to deem Requests for Admissions Set One admitted is unopposed and granted. The plaintiff has specifically waived its statutory right to monetary sanctions. A copy of this Order with Notice of Entry to which Request for Admissions which is the subject of this Order is attached shall be served on the defendant within fifteen days.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem at sbs@sbslawsf.com

Judge Pro Tem: Steven Stein, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not reported. =(302/JPT)
